description_long: This traffic barricade consists of three wide horizontal boards, each painted with alternating orange and white diagonal stripes, supported by two vertical black metal poles. The boards are evenly spaced and mounted to the poles, which are anchored to two flat, rectangular metal feet for stability. At the top of each pole, there is a small, octagonal orange reflector or warning light fixture. The overall structure is lightweight but sturdy, designed to be easily moved and placed in urban environments to block or redirect pedestrian and vehicular traffic. The surface of the boards appears to be made of painted wood or plastic, while the poles and feet are likely metal. The design is highly visible and intended to attract attention for safety purposes.
description: A highly visible traffic barricade with three orange-and-white striped boards, supported by black metal poles and flat metal feet, topped with orange reflectors or lights.
description_view_0: Front view showing three diagonal orange-and-white striped boards, two black poles, and orange reflectors on top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the boards and the depth of the metal feet, with the reflectors visible at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the striped boards, poles, and reflectors, but with less detail on the stripes' orientation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the structure of the feet and poles.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Three wide horizontal boards with diagonal orange and white stripes, stripes are of equal width and repeat across each board.
